title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: adaptiveFTS implements estimators Maissoro, Patilea & Vimond, “Adaptive prediction functional time series” — adaptive BLUP stationary functional time series observed error irregular, random design points. statistical core src/ (Rcpp + RcppArmadillo); R layer R/ orchestrates, validates inputs, cross-validation. package works release-grade: pulls caret one helper, carries undeclared parallel dependency, runtime bug disables get_nw_optimal_bw(), zero automated tests, CI. goal CRAN-acceptable, well-tested, faster package whose numerical results provably unchanged (beyond floating-point tolerance) every refactor. Top priority: numerical correctness. refactor may change estimator output beyond fp tolerance. Every change gated numerical-comparison rule .

Accept change comparison passes; otherwise revert report. Every PR touch statistical behavior shows /diff.

title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: One task = one branch --date main = one PR. Suggested branch names: chore/baseline-fixes, feat/remove-caret, perf/autocov-rcpp, test/exported-coverage, cran/check-clean, ci/actions-matrix. Commit per logical step; clear messages. Never commit main. Never change statistical behavior without showing /PR.

title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: Deps: Imports = caret, data.table, fastmatrix, MASS, methods, Rcpp, Rdpack; LinkingTo = Rcpp, RcppArmadillo; Suggests = knitr, rmarkdown. parallel used undeclared. stats used (rpois,runif) Imports. - caret → caret::createFolds R/04_estimate_mean.R:430 R/05_estimate_autocovariance.R:652 (K-fold CV index creation). - fastmatrix → kronecker.prod estimate_mean_rp (2 calls). Keep (lightweight, specialised) evaluate base-R kronecker() swap. - parallel → mclapply(..., mc.cores = 20) R/05_...:655,712 (serial Windows; hardcoded 20). Pre-existing bug: R/02_smoothing.R:243 calls get_nw_optimal_bw_cpp(..., kernel_name = ) value → get_nw_optimal_bw() errors; cascades R/07_estimate_constants.R callers. src/ hotspots (ranked): diagonal correction estimate_autocov_cpp (O(n_curve·n²·n_points) nested weight eval, 05_...:725-740); two-bandwidth estimate_autocov_risk_cpp (O(bw²·n·n_curve)); estimate_numerator_dependence_term_DD_cpp; estimate_locreg_cpp (3 NW calls per curve per t). OpenMP already bandwidth loops mean/autocov/cov-segment risk. Tests: none. CI: none. Examples: predict_curve none; estimate_cov_segment[_risk] say “Example coming soon”; format_data none; many others \dontrun. data-raw/data_far.R usethis stub, real gen code.

title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: Goal: establish reproducible baseline unblock broken paths refactor, later phases something compare . Tasks: 1. Commit PLAN.md repo root (document). 2. Capture CRAN baseline. Run R CMD build R CMD check ---cran R 4.2.1; record verbatim every ERROR/WARNING/NOTE PLAN.md (“Baseline check” appendix). 0--everything target Phase 4. 3. Fix kernel_name = bug R/02_smoothing.R:243 → pass kernel_name = kernel_name. enables behavior rather changing (function currently run). Validate calling get_nw_optimal_bw() seeded slice data_far confirming returns finite scalar; record value regression reference. 4. Declare hidden deps: add stats (temporarily) parallel Imports baseline check honest; parallel removed Phase 1. 5. Create seeded fixture script tests/testthat/helper-fixtures.R (used Phase 3 , authored now) builds small deterministic dataset (e.g. set.seed(42); simulate_far(...) subset, fixed slice data_far) reused reference captures. Files: R/02_smoothing.R, DESCRIPTION, new PLAN.md, new fixture helper. Validation: package builds; get_nw_optimal_bw() runs; baseline check recorded. Risks: R 4.2.1 older CRAN’s current release — NOTES may version-specific; ’ll re-check newer R CI (Phase 5).

title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: Goal: drop caret entirely tidy dependency list, zero change seeded results. Tasks: 1. Re-implement createFolds faithfully (exact reproduction). Add internal .create_folds(y, k, list = TRUE) new R/utils_folds.R replicates caret’s stratification + assignment algorithm , set.seed, returned fold lists identical caret’s. caret’s algorithm numeric y: cut y ~groups = min(5, ...) percentile bins, within bin assign shuffled, recycled 1:k labelling via sample(). Replicate exactly (note: y = unique(id_curve), integer 1:N, binning integers). - Validation: several seeds (N, k) combos, identical(.create_folds(...), caret::createFolds(...)) must TRUE. swap two call sites (R/04...:430, R/05...:652) confirm seeded estimate_mean_bw_rp / estimate_autocov_bw_rp outputs match Phase-0 reference via .equal(). 2. Replace parallel::mclapply (R/05...:655,712) plain lapply (deterministic, cross-platform). Optionally expose ncores/backend arg defaulting serial; kept, gate behind declared backend. Remove parallel Imports (added Phase 0). - Validation: seeded estimate_autocov_bw_rp output .equal() pre-change reference (serial vs old Windows-serial mclapply already identical; Linux confirm ). 3. Evaluate fastmatrix. Check whether fastmatrix::kronecker.prod can replaced base kronecker()/%x% identical results (.equal). yes, drop fastmatrix; perf-sensitive, keep justify. 4. Remove caret DESCRIPTION Imports; run R CMD check confirm residual references (also grep caret, parallel). Files: new R/utils_folds.R, R/04_estimate_mean.R, R/05_estimate_autocovariance.R, R/04...rp (kronecker), DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE (import tags change). Validation: dependency-removal grep clean; affected seeded estimators .equal() Phase-0 references; R CMD check new notes. Risks: caret’s createFolds internals must matched precisely — validation gate (identical fold lists) catches drift; exact match proves infeasible edge config, surface rather silently diverging.

text: , module: hoist invariants loops; pass arma objects const&; reserve()/set_size() fill loops; replace R-ish element loops bit-identical arma vectorised ops; remove redundant recomputation; exploit operator symmetry/structure; review OpenMP false sharing reduction-order safety; tighten R wrapper (avoid data.table copies, redundant format_data/gc() calls, expand.grid blowups, repeated re-smoothing). 2.1 perf/autocov-rcpp — 05_estimate_autocov_cpp.cpp (expected #1): diagonal correction src/05...:725-740 (precompute per-curve kernel weights, upper-triangle-via existing get_upper_tri_couple, kill copies); two-bandwidth estimate_autocov_risk_cpp src/05...:289-414 (cache per-bw weights & per-point regularity outside O(bw²) loop). + R/05 wrappers (estimate_autocov_bw_rp expand.grid/CV path). 2.2 perf/constants-rcpp — 07_estimate_constants_cpp.cpp, esp. estimate_numerator_dependence_term_DD_cpp / ..._single_t (DD term feeds multiple risk fns) estimate_empirical_XsXt_autocov_cpp. + R/07 wrappers. 2.3 perf/locreg-rcpp — 03_estimate_locreg_rcpp.cpp: reuse NW smooths across 3 evaluation offsets mathematically identical; cut per-t quantile re-filtering. + R/03 wrapper. 2.4 perf/mean-cov-segment-rcpp — 04_estimate_mean_cpp.cpp & 06_estimate_cov_segment_cpp.cpp (shared risk-term structure; OpenMP critical sections). + R/04, R/06 wrappers. 2.5 perf/smoothing-rcpp — 02_smoothing_rcpp.cpp: estimate_nw_cpp weight-matrix construction, estimate_nw_bw_cpp CV loop, get_nw_optimal_bw_cpp subset loop; vectorise kernels. + R/02 wrappers. 2.6 perf/curve-pipeline-rcpp — 08_estimate_curve_cpp.cpp: orchestration & helpers (ensure_positive_definite eig cost, reshape_matrix, get_nearest_best_*_bw). + R/08 predict_curve wrapper. Push remaining hot R-level loops Rcpp 2.0 justifies (speculative ports). Files per sub-phase: named src/*.cpp + .h, matching R/*.R wrapper, src/RcppExports.cpp / R/RcppExports.R (regenerated signature changes — avoid signature changes). Validation (every sub-phase, every function): seeded reference 2.0; change .equal() (fallback abs/rel tol 1e-10); validate OpenMP paths threads=1 threads>1; report /bench::mark medians. Risks: floating-point reassociation reordering sums enabling threads can exceed tolerance — keep reduction order, document order-sensitive sums, revert change fails gate.

title: adaptiveFTS — Cleanup, Performance, Tests, CRAN & CI Plan
text: Goal: testthat (edition 3) suite every exported function ≥1 test, plus regression tests lock current seeded numerics. Tasks: 1. Scaffold tests/testthat.R + tests/testthat/; add testthat Suggests; Config/testthat/edition: 3. 2. Coverage tests 38 exports (kernels, format_data, simulators, Hurst fns, estimate_locreg/mean/autocov/cov_segment + risk/rp/bw variants, estimate_sigma, empirical-moment/autocov fns, NW smoothing, predict_curve, real-data fns, exported .Qpq_fun/.Spq_fun). 3. Regression tests using Phase-0/seeded references: snapshot estimator outputs (expect_equal explicit tolerance) later phases can’t silently change results. Store refs tests/testthat/_refs/. 4. Edge cases: sparse curves (small M_n), varying per-curve M_n, boundary domain points (t 0/1), single-curve / minimal-N inputs, lag-0 vs lag>0. 5. Rcpp-path tests: call *_cpp functions directly (’re exported R) cover C++ branches (use_same_bw T/F, correct_diagonal T/F, center T/F, kernel). 6. Keep test fast (small data) example/test runtime stays CRAN-legal. Files: new tests/ tree, DESCRIPTION (Suggests + Config). Validation: devtools::test() green; covr::package_coverage() shows every export hit least . Risks: long-running estimators — use minimal fixtures precomputed bandwidths keep tests sub-second possible.

title: Adaptive Functional Time Series Analysis
text: Example mean function estimation
code: # Load data data("data_far")  # Estimate risk function for the mean dt_mean_risk <- estimate_mean_risk(   data = data_far, idcol = "id_curve", tcol = "tobs", ycol = "X",   t = c(1/4, 1/2, 3/4), bw_grid = NULL,   kernel_name = "epanechnikov" )  # Visualize mean risk at various observation points dt_dcast <- data.table::dcast(data = dt_mean_risk, formula = h ~ t, value.var = "mean_risk") manipulateWidget::combineWidgets(   list = list(     dygraphs::dygraph(       data = dt_dcast[, list(h, "t = 0.25" = `0.25`)],       main = "t = 0.25", xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function"),     dygraphs::dygraph(       data = dt_dcast[, list(h, "t = 0.5" = `0.5`)],       main = "t = 0.5", xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function"),     dygraphs::dygraph(       data = dt_dcast[, list(h, "t = 0.75" = `0.75`)],       main = "t = 0.75", xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function")   ),   nrow = 3 )  # Estimate mean function with optimal bandwidths dt_mean <- estimate_mean(   data = data_far, idcol = "id_curve", tcol = "tobs", ycol = "X",   t = c(1/4, 1/2, 3/4), bw_grid = seq(0.005, 0.15, len = 45),   kernel_name = "epanechnikov" )  # Display rounded estimates of the mean function DT::datatable(data = dt_mean[, lapply(.SD, function(X) round(X, 3))])

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load data data("data_far")  # Estimate risk function with same bandwidth for s and t dt_autocov_risk <- estimate_autocov_risk(   data = data_far, idcol = "id_curve", tcol = "tobs", ycol = "X",   s = c(1/5, 2/5, 4/5), t = c(1/4, 1/2, 3/4), lag = 1,   bw_grid = NULL, use_same_bw = TRUE, center = TRUE,   kernel_name = "epanechnikov" )  # Visualize mean risk function for different (s, t) pairs dt_dcast <- data.table::dcast(data = dt_autocov_risk,                               formula = hs ~ s + t,                               value.var = "autocov_risk") manipulateWidget::combineWidgets(   list = list(     dygraphs::dygraph(data = dt_dcast[, .(hs, "(s, t) = (0.2, 0.25)" = `0.2_0.25`)],                       main = "lag = 1 - (s, t) = (0.2, 0.25)",                       xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function"),     dygraphs::dygraph(data = dt_dcast[, .(hs, "(s, t) = (0.4, 0.5)" = `0.4_0.5`)],                       main = "lag = 1 - (s, t) = (0.4, 0.5)",                       xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function"),     dygraphs::dygraph(data = dt_dcast[, .(hs, "(s, t) = (0.8, 0.75)" = `0.8_0.75`)],                       main = "lag = 1 - (s, t) = (0.8, 0.75)",                       xlab = "h", ylab = "Risk Function")   ),   nrow = 3 )  # Estimate risk function with separate bandwidths for s and t dt_autocov_risk_2bw <- estimate_autocov_risk(   data = data_far, idcol = "id_curve", tcol = "tobs", ycol = "X",   s = c(1/5, 2/5, 4/5), t = c(1/4, 1/2, 3/4), lag = 1,   bw_grid = NULL, use_same_bw = FALSE, center = TRUE,   kernel_name = "epanechnikov" )  # Display selected columns of the results dt_autocov_risk_2bw[, .(s, t, lag, hs, ht, autocov_risk)] } # }

title: Nadaraya-Watson Kernel Estimator — estimate_nw
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Define the true regression function m <- function(t) 4 * sin(1.5 * pi * t)  # Generate observation points t <- sort(runif(n = 200, min = 0, max = 1))  # Generate measurement errors e <- rnorm(n = 200, mean = 0, sd = 0.2)  # Observed values of the regression model y <- m(t) + e  # Plot observed points and true regression function plot(x = t, y = y, main = "Observed points and true regression function") lines(x = t, y = m(t), col = "red")  # Estimate optimal bandwidth using cross-validation bw_grid <- seq(1 / (2 * length(t)), length(t) ** (-1/3), length.out = 100) hbest <- estimate_nw_bw(y = y, t = t, bw_grid = bw_grid, kernel_name = "epanechnikov")  # Estimate regression function with the Nadaraya-Watson estimator dt_nw <- estimate_nw(y = y, t = t, tnew = seq(0.01, 0.99, length.out = 100),                      h = hbest, kernel_name = "epanechnikov")  # Plot estimated and true regression functions plot(x = dt_nw[, tnew], y = dt_nw[, yhat], type = "l", col = "blue",      main = "Estimated and true regression function") lines(x = dt_nw[, tnew], y = m(dt_nw[, tnew]), col = "red") legend(x = 0.64, y = 4.1, fill = c("blue", "red"), legend = c("Estimated m", "True m")) } # }

title: adaptiveFTS 0.1.1
text: First CRAN-targeted release.

title: adaptiveFTS 0.1.1
text: Local regularity parameter estimation (estimate_locreg()). Adaptive mean function estimation (estimate_mean(), estimate_mean_risk()). Adaptive autocovariance / covariance function estimation (estimate_autocov(), estimate_autocov_risk(), estimate_cov_segment()). Adaptive Best Linear Unbiased Predictor (predict_curve()). Nadaraya–Watson smoothing bandwidth selection, kernels, simulation functional time series (FAR/FMA, fBm/mfBm) Rubin–Panaretos estimators. Numerical core implemented C++ via Rcpp/RcppArmadillo.

title: adaptiveFTS 0.1.1
text: Local regularity estimation now fully reproducible: tie-breaking jitter previously used Armadillo’s RNG (uncontrolled set.seed()) replaced deterministic offset. Substantial speed-ups C++ estimators (autocovariance risk estimation, mean covariance-segment risk) bit-identical results, validated committed regression references.

title: adaptiveFTS 0.1.1
text: Removed caret, fastmatrix parallel dependencies favour base R data.table. Added testthat (edition 3) test suite covering every exported function plus numerical regression tests. Added continuous integration (R-CMD-check Linux/macOS/Windows, test coverage, lint, pkgdown site).
